name: portion
doc: Element portion is used for describing the structure and content of an independent portion of a document

root.name: portion
root.doc: Element portion is used for describing the structure and content of an independent portion of a document

children.name: publication
children.doc: The element publication is the metadata container specifying an official publication event for the FRBR expression of the document.

children.name: references
children.doc: The element references is a metadata container of all the references to entities external to the document mentioned in the document. They include references to legal documents of any form,a s well as references to people, organizations, events, roles, concepts, and anything else is managed by the Akoma Ntoso ontology.

children.name: proprietary
children.doc: The element proprietary is a metadata container of any additional metadata property that does not belong to the Akoma Ntoso properties. Anything can be placed in this element.

children.name: presentation
children.doc: The element presentation is a metadata container of any presentation specification for the visual rendering of Akoam Ntoso elements. Anything can be placed in this element.

children.name: portionBody
children.doc: the element portionBody is the container of a portion of another document

children.name: recitals
children.doc: the element recitals is the section of the preamble that contains recitals

children.name: recital
children.doc: the element recital is the individual element of the preface that is called recital

children.name: citations
children.doc: the element citations is the section of the preamble that contains citations

children.name: citation
children.doc: the element citation is the individual element of the preface that is called citation

children.name: longTitle
children.doc: the element longTitle is the section of the preface or coverPage that is called long title

children.name: formula
children.doc: the element formula is a section of the preface or preamble that contains a formulaic expression that is systematically or frequently present in a preface or a preamble and has e precise legal meaning (e.g. an enacting formula). Use the refersTo attribute for the specification of the actual type of formula.

children.name: coverPage
children.doc: the element coverPage is used as a container of the text that acts as a cover page

children.name: preface
children.doc: the element preface is used as a container of all prefacing material (e.g. headers, formulas, etc.)

children.name: preamble
children.doc: the element preamble is used as a container of the text that opens the main body of the document as a preamble

children.name: container
children.doc: the element container is a generic element for a container.

children.name: administrationOfOath
children.doc: this element is a structural container for parts of a debates that contain the administration of an oath

children.name: rollCall
children.doc: this element is a structural container for parts of a debates that contain a roll call of individuals

children.name: prayers
children.doc: this element is a structural container for parts of a debates that contain prayers

children.name: oralStatements
children.doc: this element is a structural container for parts of a debates that contain oral statements by participants

children.name: writtenStatements
children.doc: this element is a structural container for parts of a debates that contain written statements by participants

children.name: personalStatements
children.doc: this element is a structural container for parts of a debates that contain written statements by participants

children.name: ministerialStatements
children.doc: this element is a structural container for parts of a debates that contain written statements by participants

children.name: resolutions
children.doc: this element is a structural container for parts of a debates that contain resolutions

children.name: nationalInterest
children.doc: this element is a structural container for parts of a debates that contain resolutions

children.name: declarationOfVote
children.doc: this element is a structural container for parts of a debates that are relevant to the declaration of votes

children.name: communication
children.doc: this element is a structural container for parts of a debates that contain communications from the house

children.name: petitions
children.doc: this element is a structural container for parts of a debates that are relevant to petitions

children.name: papers
children.doc: this element is a structural container for parts of a debates that are relevant to the display of papers

children.name: noticesOfMotion
children.doc: this element is a structural container for parts of a debates that are relevant to the notices of motions

children.name: questions
children.doc: this element is a structural container for parts of a debates that are relevant to questions

children.name: address
children.doc: this element is a structural container for parts of a debates that are relevant to addresses

children.name: proceduralMotions
children.doc: this element is a structural container for parts of a debates that are relevant to procedural motions

children.name: pointOfOrder
children.doc: this element is a structural container for parts of a debates that are relevant to points of order

children.name: adjournment
children.doc: this element is a structural container for parts of a debates that contain adjournment notices

children.name: debateSection
children.doc: this element is a generic structural container for all other parts of a debates that are not explicitly supported with a named element

children.name: div
children.doc: The element div is an HTML element, but is NOT used in Akoma Ntoso as in HTML. Instead of being used as a generic block, Akoma Ntoso uses div as a generic container (as in common practice)

children.name: hcontainer
children.doc: the element hcontainer is a generic element for a hierarchical container. It can be placed in a hierarchy instead of any of the other hierarchical containers. The attribute name is required and gives a name to the element.

children.name: clause
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "clause" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: section
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "section" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: part
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "part" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: paragraph
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "paragraph" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: chapter
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "chapter" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: title
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "title" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: article
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "article" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: book
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "book" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: tome
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "tome" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: division
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "division" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: list
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "list" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: point
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "point" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: indent
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "indent" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: alinea
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "alinea" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: rule
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "rule" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: subrule
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "subrule" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: proviso
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "proviso" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: subsection
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "subsection" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: subpart
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "subpart" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: subparagraph
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "subparagraph" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: subchapter
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "subchapter" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: subtitle
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "subtitle" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: subdivision
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "subdivision" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: subclause
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "subclause" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: sublist
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "sublist" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: level
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "level" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: transitional
children.doc: this element is a hierarchical container called "transitional" either explicitly or due to the local tradition

children.name: speechGroup
children.doc: the element speechGroup is a container of speech elements. This element is meant to pooint out, in a complex sequence of individual speech elements, the main contributor, i.e., the individual speech who was introducedand expected and that is causing the complex sequence that follows. Attributes by, as and to are those of the main speech.

children.name: speech
children.doc: the element speech is a container of a single speech utterance in a debate. Dialogs between speakers need a speech element each

children.name: question
children.doc: the element question is a container of a single official question as proposed by an MP to a person holding an official position

children.name: answer
children.doc: the element answer is a container of a single official answer to a question

children.name: other
children.doc: the element other is a container of parts of a debate that are not speeches, nor scene comments (e.g., lists of papers, etc.)

children.name: scene
children.doc: the element scene is a container of descriptions of the scene elements happening in a given moment during a debate (e.g., applauses)

children.name: narrative
children.doc: the element narrative is a block element in a debate to mark description in the third person of events taking place in the meeting, e.g. "Mr X. takes the Chair"

children.name: summary
children.doc: the element summary is a block element in a debate to mark summaries of speeches that are individually not interesting (e.g.: "Question put and agreed to")
